title: English Grammar, Reading & Story Composition
subject: English Language Arts & Reading
description: Master parts of speech (nouns, verbs, adjectives), punctuation, paragraph structure, and creative story writing.
learningObjectives: Identify Nouns, Verbs, Adjectives, and Adverbs in sentences.
Apply proper capitalization and punctuation (periods, commas, quotation marks).
Compose a narrative paragraph with a topic sentence, supporting details, and a conclusion.

lessons.title: Parts of Speech: Nouns, Verbs & Adjectives
lessons.content: A NOUN names a person, place, or thing (e.g., 'forest', 'ranger'). A VERB expresses action (e.g., 'runs', 'searches'). An ADJECTIVE describes a noun (e.g., 'vibrant', 'thick').

lessons.title: Structuring a Great Paragraph
lessons.content: Every paragraph needs 3 key parts: 1) A Topic Sentence (introduces main idea), 2) Supporting Sentences (provides evidence and detail), and 3) A Concluding Sentence (wraps up the thought).

flashcards.definition: A word that names a person, place, thing, or idea.
An action word that tells what a subject is doing.
A word that describes or modifies a noun.
The sentence that summarizes the main point of a paragraph.

questions.question: In the sentence 'The brave ranger explored the dark cave,' which word is the ADJECTIVE describing the ranger?
What is the primary role of a topic sentence in paragraph writing?
options: To state the main idea of the paragraph

practicalChallenge.title: Field Journal Story Writer
practicalChallenge.instruction: Write a 5-sentence paragraph describing an outdoor adventure. Underline all Nouns in blue and circle all Verbs in green!
